message:
perf(sync,scheduler,net): migrate Mutex from std to parking_lot across pool, rate limiter, socket, and three-lane scheduler

Replaces std::sync::Mutex with parking_lot::Mutex in four subsystems that
previously required .expect("lock poisoned") or .unwrap() on every lock
acquisition:

- GenericPool: state, return_rx, return_wakers all become parking_lot::Mutex.
  PooledResource::notify_return_wakers no longer needs a poisoned-lock bailout
  path in Drop—parking_lot simply never poisons. The try_lock() API returns
  Option instead of Result, so is_ok() → is_some() throughout.

- RateLimit: single state Mutex migrated; 8 .expect("rate limit lock poisoned")
  calls and 4 .unwrap() calls in tests removed.

- TcpSocket: state Mutex migrated; into_inner() no longer needs
  unwrap_or_else(PoisonError::into_inner) since parking_lot::Mutex::into_inner
  always succeeds.

- ThreeLaneScheduler: introduces LocalReadyQueue type alias
  (ParkingMutex<Vec<TaskId>>) for the per-worker non-stealable local queues.
  All .expect("local_ready lock poisoned") calls removed. try_lock() returns
  Option, matching is_ok_and() → direct method call. ~40 test sites updated.

- cx/scope.rs: test-only Mutex usage updated to parking_lot.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
